attractions.name: Alhambra RV Park (Site of Historic Alhambra Hot Springs)
attractions.description: Alhambra RV Park is located on the historic site of the Alhambra and Sunnyside Sanatoriums, which once drew visitors from across the country to soak in natural hot springs. While the original resort facilities were destroyed by fire in 1959, the park now offers a serene camping experience along the Prickly Pear stream with modern amenities and a historic atmosphere. (Alhambra RV Park, Ultimate Montana)
attractions.hours: Office/Store: Open daily during season; Staff: 24-hour on-site assistance available (Alhambra RV Park)
attractions.admission: Day Use: $5.00; Tent Sites: $15.00; RV Sites: $25.00 - $55.00 per night depending on hookups and day of the week (Alhambra RV Park Rates)
highlights: 'Located on the historic site of early 20th-century hot spring sanitariums ([Alhambra RV Park)', 'Scenic campsites situated directly along the Prickly Pear stream (Tripadvisor)', 'Opportunities for trout fishing and wildlife viewing on the property (Hipcamp)', 'Proximity to Helena and major regional attractions like Tizer Botanic Gardens (Southwest Montana)', 'Access to miles of nearby hiking, biking, and OHV trails (Alhambra RV Park)']
attractions.tips: The original hot springs are no longer open for public soaking as the water was found to be radioactive and is now used for private heating (MTPR). Visitors should book reservations in advance via RoverPass to ensure availability during the peak summer season (RoverPass).
attractions.nearby: Tizer Botanic Gardens, Lewis & Clark County Fairgrounds, Helena State Capital, Montana City, and various historic ghost towns (Alhambra RV Park Attractions)

attractions.name: Archie Bray Foundation for the Ceramic Arts
attractions.description: The Archie Bray Foundation for the Ceramic Arts is a world-renowned nonprofit educational institution and artist residency program dedicated to the enrichment of ceramic arts. Founded in 1951 on the site of the historic Western Clay Manufacturing Company, the 26-acre campus features working studios, exhibition galleries, and a vast collection of outdoor sculptures scattered among historic brickyard remains. (Archie Bray Foundation, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Office: Tue–Fri 11am–5pm (By Appointment); Gallery & Studios: Tue–Fri 11am–5pm; Bray Clay Business: Mon–Fri 10am–5pm, Sat 9am–12pm & 1pm–5pm; Grounds: Daily during daylight hours (Archie Bray Foundation, Tripadvisor)
attractions.days_open: Grounds: Daily; Galleries/Business: Monday–Saturday (varies by department) (Archie Bray Foundation)
attractions.admission: Free (Galleries and grounds are open to the public at no cost) (Visit Helena, Tripadvisor)
highlights: 'Internationally acclaimed ceramic artist residency program ([Wikipedia)', '26-acre historic campus listed on the National Register of Historic Places (Archie Bray Foundation)', 'Extensive collection of site-specific outdoor ceramic sculptures and artifacts (Tripadvisor)', 'Historic beehive kilns from the former brickyard (Archie Bray Foundation)', 'Year-round exhibition and sales galleries featuring resident work (Archie Bray Foundation)', 'Community ceramic classes and specialized workshops for all ages (Tripadvisor)']
attractions.tips: Take a self-guided walking tour of the grounds; maps are available at the site. Be respectful of resident artists working in their studios. Visit during daylight hours to fully appreciate the outdoor sculptures and historic brickyard structures. (Tripadvisor, Archie Bray Gallery)
attractions.nearby: Spring Meadow Lake State Park, MontanaWILD, Broadwater Hot Springs, and Last Chance Gulch (Downtown Helena) (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Beartooth Wildlife Management Area (accessible via Beartooth Road)
attractions.description: The Beartooth Wildlife Management Area is a 35,000-acre conservation area north of Helena that provides critical winter range for elk, mule deer, and bighorn sheep. It offers rugged terrain for outdoor enthusiasts, featuring intermountain grasslands, ponderosa pine forests, and stunning views of the nearby 'Sleeping Giant' mountain. Visit Montana, University of Montana
attractions.region: Lewis and Clark County (North of Helena) Southwest Montana
attractions.hours: Open 24/7 from noon on May 15 through November 30; Closed to all public access from December 1 to May 14. Montana FWP
attractions.admission: Free entry (Visitors 12+ must possess a valid Montana Conservation License: $8 Resident, $10 Nonresident) Montana FWP
highlights: 'Spectacular wildlife viewing including elk, bighorn sheep, and mule deer. [University of Montana', 'Unrivaled views of Beartooth Mountain, popularly known as the "Sleeping Giant." Big Sky Walker', 'Access to hiking trails, horseback riding, and primitive backcountry camping. Montana FWP', 'Birdwatching opportunities for Bald Eagles, Trumpeter Swans, and Lewis’s Woodpeckers. Southwest Montana', 'Direct access to the Missouri River and Holter Lake for boating and fishing. Visit Montana']
attractions.tips: There is no potable water on-site, so visitors must bring their own; the area operates under 'Pack-In, Pack-Out' principles with no trash service. Be aware that the gravel access roads can become very slick and impassable for some vehicles when wet. Montana Outdoor
attractions.nearby: Holter Lake, Gates of the Mountains Wilderness, Wolf Creek, Missouri River Visit Montana

attractions.name: Beattie Park
attractions.description: Beattie Park is a triangular city block of approximately 0.6 acres located adjacent to the historic Union Station in Helena. It serves as an extension of the depot area and is notable for featuring a retired 1902 steam locomotive on permanent display (National Register of Historic Places, official site).
attractions.region: Railroad District (Sixth Ward) (National Register of Historic Places, City of Helena)
attractions.hours: Open daily from dawn to dusk (Airial Travel)
attractions.admission: Free (Airial Travel)
highlights: 'Northern Pacific Steam Locomotive 1382 display ([Trainorders.com)', 'Historic Union Station (Helena Railroad Depot) adjacent (National Register of Historic Places)', 'Picnic shelter and tables (official site)', 'Shaded respite with mature trees and shrubs (National Register of Historic Places)', '1931 Granite bench dedicated by the American War Mothers (National Register of Historic Places)', 'Part of the National Register of Historic Places Railroad Depot District (National Register of Historic Places)']
attractions.tips: Great for train enthusiasts and families with children who want to see the historic steam engine; bring a camera for photos of the locomotive and the adjacent historic depot (Trainorders.com, Enjoy Your Parks).
attractions.nearby: Historic Union Station, Montana State Capitol, Last Chance Gulch, Reeder's Alley (National Register of Historic Places, Reddit)

attractions.name: Blackfoot River Brewing Company (official site)
attractions.description: Founded in 1998 by three passionate homebrewers, Blackfoot River Brewing is a staple of downtown Helena known for its handcrafted, unfiltered beers. The brewery emphasizes traditional brewing methods and quality ingredients, serving a diverse lineup of draught-only selections in a welcoming, multi-level taproom environment (official site).
attractions.hours: Winter Hours: Mon-Thurs 2:00 PM - 9:00 PM, Fri-Sun 12:00 PM - 9:00 PM; Summer Hours (beginning June 1st): Open until 10:00 PM daily (official site).
attractions.admission: Free (official site)
highlights: 'Handcrafted, unfiltered, and unpasteurized beers available exclusively on draught ([official site)', 'Multi-level taproom featuring a second-floor deck with views of the surrounding mountains (official site, Yelp)', 'Free popcorn for all visitors, a beloved local tradition (official site, Tripadvisor)', 'Traditional Beer Engine for serving cask-conditioned real ales (official site)', 'Pet-friendly downstairs outdoor areas and seasonal parklet (official site)', 'Active community involvement through "Community Night" events and local sponsorships (official site)']
attractions.tips: Montana state law limits taproom consumption to 48 oz (three pints) per person per day (Yelp). While the brewery only serves popcorn, visitors are welcome to bring in outside food from nearby restaurants like Park Avenue Bakery or neighboring pizza shops (official site, Yelp). The taproom operates on an honor system where you pay for your total order before leaving (Yelp).
attractions.nearby: Reeder's Alley, Mount Helena City Park, Last Chance Gulch, State Capitol Building, Holter Museum of Art (Yelp, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Broadwater Hot Springs & Fitness
attractions.description: Broadwater Hot Springs & Fitness is a historic family-owned wellness destination in Helena, Montana, that has served the community for over a century. The facility features 25,000 square feet of amenities, including multiple natural mineral soaking pools, a full-service fitness floor, and a relaxing spa. Visitors can also enjoy local food and craft beer at The Springs Taproom & Grill, which frequently hosts live music events. Broadwater Hot Springs Official Site
attractions.region: Southwest Helena / Ten Mile Creek Area
attractions.hours: Hot Springs & Fitness: Mon-Thurs 6:00 AM - 10:30 PM, Fri 6:00 AM - 11:30 PM, Sat 7:30 AM - 11:30 PM, Sun 7:30 AM - 10:30 PM. Taproom & Grill: Mon-Thurs 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Fri 11:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Sat 9:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM - 10:00 PM. Broadwater Hot Springs Hours
attractions.admission: Mon-Thurs: Adults $17, Kids (0-4) $8, Kids (5-13) $15, Seniors (65+) $15, Military/Teachers/First Responders $15. Fri-Sun & Holidays: Adults $22, Kids (0-4) $12, Kids (5-13) $20, Seniors (65+) $17, Military/Teachers/First Responders $17. Broadwater Hot Springs Day Rates
highlights: ['Natural mineral hot springs with five hot pools and two cool pools for contrast therapy.', 'Full-service 25,000 sq ft fitness facility offering group classes like yoga and muscle max.', 'The Springs Taproom & Grill providing poolside food and beverage service.', 'Amenities including a sauna, steam room, and year-round outdoor soaking.', 'Regular live music performances on an outdoor stage.', 'Newly renovated locker rooms and state-of-the-art fitness equipment.']
attractions.tips: No re-entry is allowed once you enter with a single admission pass. Pools close 15 minutes before the facility's closing time. No reservations are required to visit. Note that a 3% processing fee is added to all soaking credit transactions. Broadwater Hot Springs FAQ
attractions.nearby: Cathedral of St. Helena, Reeder's Alley, Mount Helena City Park, Archie Bray Foundation for the Ceramic Arts, and Last Chance Gulch Walking Mall. Southwest Montana Attractions

attractions.name: Canyon Ferry Reservoir (Canyon Ferry Lake Recreation Area)
attractions.description: Canyon Ferry Reservoir is a massive 35,000-acre body of water located approximately 20 miles east of Helena, offering 76 miles of shoreline for diverse outdoor activities. Managed by the Bureau of Reclamation, it serves as a premier destination for fishing, boating, and camping, while also providing flood control and hydroelectric power for the region. Visit Montana, Enjoy Your Parks
attractions.hours: Recreation area: Open 24 hours daily. Visitor Center: Seasonal (typically Memorial Day to Labor Day), daily 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM. Recreation.gov, Ultimate Montana
attractions.admission: Free for general day use; Camping fees range from $10 to $20 per night for standard sites, and up to $35 for group use areas. Bureau of Reclamation, Recreation.gov
highlights: 'Excellent fishing opportunities for rainbow trout, perch, ling, and walleye [Bureau of Reclamation', '8 developed campgrounds and multiple day-use areas with concrete boat ramps Recreation.gov', 'Seasonal visitor center with informative and interesting exhibits Ultimate Montana', 'Three marina concessions providing rental docks and small stores Recreation.gov', 'Stunning views of the Big Belt and Elkhorn Mountains Enjoy Your Parks']
attractions.tips: Watch for deer on the roads when driving to the area; the west shore is best for picnicking and swimming, while the east shore has more campgrounds. Tripadvisor
attractions.nearby: Canyon Ferry Dam, Gates of the Mountains, Montana State Capitol, Cathedral of Saint Helena, Broadwater Hot Springs. Enjoy Your Parks, Reddit

attractions.name: Carroll College (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: Carroll College is a private Catholic liberal arts college founded in 1909. Located in Montana's capital, it is well-regarded for its pre-professional programs in nursing, engineering, and biology, and its campus offers stunning views of the Rocky Mountains (Wikipedia, Carroll College).
attractions.address: 1601 N Benton Ave, Helena, MT 59625 (Carroll College)
attractions.hours: Monday – Friday: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM (Visitor/Business Office); Campus tours leave daily at 10:00 AM and 1:00 PM (Carroll College, Carroll College)
attractions.admission: Free for campus visitors and prospective student tours (Carroll College, Roaming Near and Far)
highlights: 'Neuman Observatory, the oldest astronomical observatory in Montana ([Wikipedia)', 'All Saints Chapel, a central landmark on the 63-acre campus (Wikipedia)', 'Anthrozoology program, the first undergraduate degree of its kind in the U.S. (Wikipedia)', 'Fighting Saints athletic programs with 15 NAIA sports teams (Wikipedia)', 'Scenic campus views of Mount Helena and the surrounding Rocky Mountains (Carroll College)']
attractions.tips: Prospective students should schedule an individual visit to tour residence halls and the dining hall; the campus is particularly scenic in winter when decorated with Christmas lights (Carroll College, Roaming Near and Far).
attractions.nearby: Cathedral of St. Helena, Mount Helena City Park, Reeder's Alley, Montana State Capitol, Spring Meadow Lake State Park (Roaming Near and Far)

attractions.name: Cathedral of Saint Helena (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: The Cathedral of Saint Helena is a stunning neo-Gothic cathedral modeled after the Votivkirche in Vienna, Austria. Completed in 1914, it serves as the seat of the Roman Catholic Diocese of Helena and is distinguished by its 230-foot twin spires that overlook the city. The interior features 59 magnificent stained glass windows, white marble altars, and hand-carved oak woodwork. (Official Website, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: 530 N. Ewing St., Helena, MT 59601 (Official Website)
attractions.hours: Monday-Friday: 6:30 AM - 6:00 PM; Saturday: 9:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Sunday: 7:00 AM - 3:00 PM (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Official Website)
highlights: "230-foot twin spires inspired by Vienna's Votivkirche ([Wikipedia)", '59 stained glass windows depicting biblical scenes from the Old and New Testaments (Official Website)', 'White marble altars and statues carved from pure Carrara marble (Visit Montana)', 'Listed on the National Register of Historic Places since 1980 (Wikipedia)', 'Fifteen hand-cast bells in the North tower representing the mysteries of the Rosary (Wikipedia)', 'Intricate hand-carved oak pews and woodwork (Visit Montana)']
attractions.tips: Visitors are welcome outside of liturgy times but are asked to be respectful of parishioners; free guided tours are available by appointment for groups of 10 or more, while summer walk-in tours (Memorial Day to Labor Day) occur Tuesday through Thursday from 1:00 PM to 3:00 PM. (Official Website)
attractions.nearby: Montana State Capitol, Original Governor's Mansion, Reeder’s Alley, Last Chance Gulch, Holter Museum of Art (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Centennial/Bausch Park (Centennial Park)
attractions.description: Centennial/Bausch Park is a versatile 58-acre community space located on the west side of North Main Street. It serves as a central hub for recreation in Helena, offering a wide array of amenities including sports fields, paved trails, and specialized areas for dogs and skaters (City of Helena). The park is well-known for its impressive American flag monument and its scenic views of the surrounding mountains (Airial Travel).
attractions.region: North Central Neighborhood (Coldwell Banker Mountainside Realty)
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily (Airial Travel, Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: Free (Airial Travel, Visit Helena)
highlights: 'Expansive sports fields for softball, soccer, and baseball ([City of Helena)', 'Off-leash dog park with picturesque mountain views (Airial Travel)', 'Paved jogging and walking paths that connect to the regional Centennial Trail system (HelenaOutdoors)', 'On-site skate park and a dedicated bike track for cycling enthusiasts (City of Helena)', 'Features one of the largest American flags and flagpoles in the United States (Airial Travel)', 'Community gardens and a large picnic pavilion available for reservations (City of Helena)']
attractions.tips: The park features a pedestrian underpass beneath Highway 12, allowing safe access to the Great Northern Town Center and Carousel. It is highly recommended for picnics due to its numerous tables and large pavilion, and dog owners should utilize the designated off-leash area at the north end. Visitors suggest that some playground equipment is better suited for older children, so parents of toddlers should monitor their play closely (Airial Travel).
attractions.nearby: Carroll College, Great Northern Town Center, ExplorationWorks, Great Northern Carousel, Last Chance Gulch Walking Mall, Bill Roberts Golf Course (Coldwell Banker Mountainside Realty, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: City of Helena Wastewater Treatment Plant
attractions.description: The City of Helena's Wastewater Treatment Facility (WWTF) is a modern utility that utilizes a biological nutrient removal (BNR) process to treat over 1 billion gallons of wastewater annually. It plays a critical role in protecting public health and the environment by removing pollutants before discharging treated water into Prickly Pear Creek.
attractions.region: Northeast Helena / East Custer Avenue area
attractions.hours: Sunday: Closed, Monday: 08:00 AM–05:00 PM, Tuesday: 08:00 AM–05:00 PM, Wednesday: 08:00 AM–05:00 PM, Thursday: 08:00 AM–05:00 PM, Friday: 08:00 AM–05:00 PM, Saturday: Closed
attractions.admission: Free (Not open to the public; tours by request)
highlights: ['Biological Nutrient Removal (BNR) process for nitrogen and phosphorus removal', 'Processes over 1 billion gallons of wastewater annually', 'Discharges treated effluent into Prickly Pear Creek, supporting the Missouri River watershed', 'Utilizes byproduct methane gas for heating needs', 'Equipped with ultra-violet (UV) light disinfection for process and irrigation water reuse', 'Includes a regional industrial pretreatment program to manage industrial waste']
attractions.tips: The facility is not generally open for public walk-ins, but educational tours for groups or individuals can be scheduled by contacting the Public Works Department. Residents are encouraged to reduce phosphorus-containing household products to assist in water quality efforts for the Lake Helena sub-watershed.
attractions.nearby: Prickly Pear Creek, Helena Regional Airport, Last Chance Gulch (Downtown Helena), Montana State Capitol

attractions.name: Clark Fork River (Missoula)
attractions.description: The Clark Fork River is the largest river in Montana by volume, serving as the heart of Missoula's recreational and cultural life as it flows directly through the downtown corridor. Once a vital industrial waterway, it has undergone extensive restoration to become a premier destination for world-class fly fishing, whitewater kayaking at Brennan’s Wave, and scenic floating. The river is flanked by extensive trail systems and parks, making it a central hub for both residents and visitors seeking outdoor adventure. City of Missoula, Engage Missoula
attractions.address: 101 Carousel Drive, Missoula, MT 59802 (Caras Park Access)
attractions.region: Missoula County / Five Valleys Region (Approx. 115 miles west of Helena) USGS
attractions.hours: Daily: 6:00 AM - 11:00 PM (Standard Missoula City Park hours for riverfront access) City of Missoula Municipal Code
attractions.admission: Free (Fishing requires a Montana fishing license for those 12 and older) Montana FWP
attractions.phone: 406-721-7275 (Missoula Parks & Recreation Office) City of Missoula
highlights: 'Brennan’s Wave: A man-made whitewater wave in downtown Missoula popular for river surfing and kayaking. [Destination Missoula', 'Caras Park: A major downtown hub located along the riverbanks that hosts festivals, food trucks, and community events. Missoula Parks & Recreation', 'Kim Williams Nature Trail: A scenic 4.2-mile riverside trail used for walking, biking, and wildlife viewing. City of Missoula', 'World-Class Fly Fishing: Renowned for its populations of rainbow, brown, and Westslope cutthroat trout. Montana Fishing Outfitters', 'River Floating: A quintessential Missoula summer activity, with popular routes passing through the heart of the city. Gather and Go Travel', 'Milltown State Park: A nearby 635-acre park at the confluence of the Clark Fork and Blackfoot Rivers, reclaimed from a former Superfund site. Montana FWP']
attractions.tips: Use designated river access points like the new 'Beartracks' or 'Toole Park' stairs to prevent bank erosion. Always check river flow levels and water temperatures before floating, and be sure to wear a life jacket. Anglers must possess a valid Montana fishing license and adhere to regional catch limits. Engage Missoula, Montana FWP
attractions.nearby: University of Montana, Mount Sentinel (The "M" Trail), Missoula Art Museum, Dragon Hollow Playarea, Montgomery Distillery. Destination Missoula

attractions.name: Delta Hotels by Marriott Helena Colonial (The Colonial)
attractions.description: The Delta Hotels Helena Colonial is a landmark full-service hotel and one of Montana's largest convention facilities, widely known in the region as "The Colonial." It features over 18,000 square feet of versatile meeting and ballroom space, serving as a primary hub for large-scale conferences, community events, and entertainment in the state capital. Situated near the Montana State Capitol, the property offers modern amenities including an on-site restaurant and lounge (Visit Helena, Marriott).
attractions.hours: Hotel: 24/7; Colonial Restaurant: 6:30 AM - 10:00 PM Daily; Colonial Lounge: 4:00 PM - 11:00 PM Daily (Marriott)
attractions.admission: Free (for property entry); Event tickets and room rates vary (Visit Helena, Marriott)
highlights: 'Over 18,000 square feet of flexible meeting and ballroom space for events ([Marriott)', 'On-site dining at the Colonial Restaurant and drinks at the Colonial Lounge (Marriott)', 'Located just blocks from the Montana State Capitol and state offices (Visit Helena)', 'Features a heated indoor pool, whirlpool, and modern fitness center (Marriott)', 'Complimentary shuttle service to and from Helena Regional Airport (Marriott)', 'Historic local significance as a long-standing gathering place in Helena (Helena History)']
attractions.tips: The venue is a major host for state-wide conventions and community performances; check local calendars for upcoming events in the ballroom. The on-site lounge is a popular local spot for happy hour in a relaxed setting (Visit Helena, Marriott).
attractions.nearby: Montana State Capitol, Cathedral of St. Helena, Reeder's Alley, Great Northern Carousel, Mount Helena City Park (Visit Helena)

attractions.name: Equity Fountain (Sphere of Interconnectedness)
attractions.description: The Equity Fountain is a prominent public art installation and functional fountain in Helena's Hill Park, installed in 2020 to replace the city's controversial Confederate Memorial Fountain. Titled the "Sphere of Interconnectedness," the fountain symbolizes unity, diversity, and community healing. It features a stainless steel sphere resting on a massive millstone base, serving as a peaceful landmark in the heart of downtown Helena (City of Helena, Studiofolia).
attractions.hours: Monday: 5:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Tuesday: 5:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Wednesday: 5:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Thursday: 5:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Friday: 5:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 5:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 5:00 AM – 11:00 PM (City of Helena)
attractions.admission: Free (City of Helena)
highlights: ['Symbolic Replacement: Replaced the only Confederate monument in the Northwestern U.S. in a move for community equity.', '"Sphere of Interconnectedness": Features a four-foot stainless steel sphere with woven metal strands representing human connections.', 'Multilingual Engravings: Inscribed with universal values like "Respect," "Equity," and "Peace" in English and Native American languages.', 'Native American Wisdom: Incorporates the "Teachings of the Seven Grandfathers," a traditional value system.', 'Massive Millstone Base: Sits on a 4,500-pound granite millstone base sourced from Maine.', 'Collaborative Design: Created by landscape designer James Dinh and metal sculptor Michael Stutz.']
attractions.tips: Visit in the late afternoon to see the sun reflect off the stainless steel sphere, and take a moment to read the interpretive signage that explains the site's transition from the former 1916 memorial to the current fountain (City of Helena).
attractions.nearby: Last Chance Gulch Walking Mall, Lewis & Clark County Library, Helena Civic Center, Cathedral of St. Helena, Reeder's Alley (Visit Helena)

attractions.name: Fire Tower Coffee House
attractions.description: Fire Tower Coffee House is a warm and welcoming space in the heart of downtown Helena that has been owned by Nord Johnson since 2013. The shop is known for its eclectic atmosphere, featuring organic beans roasted in-house and a menu of made-to-order breakfast and lunch dishes, including gluten-free and vegetarian options (Fire Tower Coffee).
attractions.address: 422 N. Last Chance Gulch (Fire Tower Coffee)
attractions.region: Downtown / Last Chance Gulch (Fire Tower Coffee)
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 8:00 am – 2:00 pm, Saturday: 8:00 am – 2:00 pm, Sunday: CLOSED (Fire Tower Coffee)
attractions.admission: Free (Fire Tower Coffee)
highlights: 'Organic beans roasted in-house ([Fire Tower Coffee)', 'Eclectic atmosphere with signed guitars and album collections (Fire Tower Coffee)', 'Made-to-order breakfast and lunch dishes (Fire Tower Coffee)', 'Sustainable coffee practices prioritized (Fire Tower Coffee)', 'Gluten-free and vegetarian options available (Fire Tower Coffee)', 'Located in historic downtown Helena (Fire Tower Coffee)']
attractions.tips: A great spot for a relaxed coffee break in a unique, music-themed environment; try local favorites like 'Dave's Burrito' or the 'Caitlin' sandwich (Fire Tower Coffee). Some visitors note that the shop may close early if it is a slow day (Yelp).
attractions.nearby: Last Chance Gulch Walking Mall, Reeder’s Alley, Cathedral of St. Helena, Montana State Capitol, Montana Historical Society Museum (Tripadvisor, Visit Helena)

attractions.name: Gates of the Mountains Boat Tours & Marina (Gates of the Mountains)
attractions.description: The Gates of the Mountains boat tour offers a scenic two-hour cruise along the Missouri River, following the path explored by Meriwether Lewis in 1805. Visitors glide through 1,200-foot-high limestone cliffs and rugged rock formations while learning about the area's rich history, including the Lewis and Clark expedition and the Mann Gulch fire. (Gates of the Mountains)
attractions.address: 3131 Gates of the Mountains Rd, Helena, MT 59602 (Gates of the Mountains)
attractions.hours: May 23 - May 25: 10:00 AM, 12:00 PM, 2:00 PM; May 26 - June 30: Weekdays 11:00 AM, 2:00 PM, Weekends 10:00 AM, 12:00 PM, 2:00 PM; July 1 - August 31: 10:00 AM, 12:00 PM, 2:00 PM; September 1 - September 13: Weekdays 11:00 AM, 2:00 PM, Weekends 10:00 AM, 12:00 PM, 2:00 PM (Closed Mon/Tue except Labor Day) (Gates of the Mountains)
attractions.days_open: Daily (May 23 – August 31); Wednesday – Sunday (September 1 – September 13) (Gates of the Mountains)
attractions.admission: Adult (18-59): $20; Senior (60+): $15; Child (3-17): $10; Active Military (with ID): $15; 2 and Under: Free (Gates of the Mountains)
highlights: '1,200-foot-high limestone cliffs and canyon scenery ([NPS)', 'Historical sites including Mann Gulch and Indian pictographs (NPS)', 'Wildlife viewing opportunities for bald eagles, bighorn sheep, and mountain goats (YouTube)', 'Choice between open-air vintage boat (Sacajawea II) and modern covered boat (Canyon Voyager) (Travel Bug)', 'Narrated tours with insights into local geology and wildlife (Passrider)']
attractions.tips: Arrive early as same-day tickets are only available in person; pets are not allowed on tour boats. The Canyon Voyager has an onboard restroom and is ADA accessible with a ramp, while the Sacajawea II stops at a picnic area for facilities. (Gates of the Mountains, YouTube)
attractions.nearby: Holter Lake, Meriwether Picnic Area, Helena National Forest, Great Falls (Gates of the Mountains, Lewis and Clark Trail Experience)

attractions.name: Gates of the Mountains Wilderness (Gates of the Mountains)
attractions.description: The Gates of the Mountains Wilderness is a 28,562-acre natural preserve where the Missouri River carves through 1,200-foot limestone cliffs that appear to open and close like gates. Named by explorer Meriwether Lewis in 1805, this dramatic canyon offers a historic and scenic journey through one of Montana's most iconic landscapes. Visitors typically experience the area via narrated boat tours that showcase the region's unique geology, diverse wildlife, and rich cultural history. (Gates of the Mountains, Wilderness.net)
attractions.address: 3131 Gates of the Mountains Rd., Helena, MT 59602 (Gates of the Mountains)
attractions.region: Central Montana / Missouri River Corridor (Gates of the Mountains)
attractions.hours: May 23 - June 30: Weekdays 11:00 AM, 2:00 PM; Weekends 10:00 AM, 12:00 PM, 2:00 PM. July 1 - Aug 31: Daily 10:00 AM, 12:00 PM, 2:00 PM. Sept 1 - Sept 13: Weekdays 11:00 AM, 2:00 PM; Weekends 10:00 AM, 12:00 PM, 2:00 PM; Closed Mon/Tue except Labor Day. (Gates of the Mountains)
attractions.days_open: Seasonal (Late May to Mid-September) (Gates of the Mountains)
attractions.admission: Adult (18-59): $20, Senior (60+): $15, Child (3-17): $10, Active Military: $15, 2 and Under: Free (Gates of the Mountains)
highlights: 'Narrated 120-minute boat tour through the towering limestone canyon walls ([Gates of the Mountains)', 'View of Mann Gulch, the historic site of the tragic 1949 smokejumper forest fire (Gates of the Mountains)', "Ancient indigenous pictographs visible on the canyon's rock faces (Gates of the Mountains)", 'Wildlife viewing opportunities for bald eagles, osprey, and mountain goats (Gates of the Mountains)', 'Follows the historic exploration route of the Lewis and Clark Expedition (NPS)', 'Meriwether Picnic Area accessible by boat for hiking and swimming (AllTrips)']
attractions.tips: Same-day tickets are only available for purchase in person at the marina. Dogs are not permitted on the boat tours. It is highly recommended to book in advance for weekend tours and during the peak July-August season. The marina is located about 20 miles north of Helena and 3 miles off Interstate 15. (Gates of the Mountains, Reddit)
attractions.nearby: Black Sandy State Park, Holter Lake, Missouri River, Mount Helena, Helena National Forest, Lewis & Clark Brewing Co. (Southwest Montana, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Golden Girls Antique Mall
attractions.description: Golden Girls Antiques Mall is Helena's largest antique shopping destination, featuring 10,000 square feet of space with over 50 individual dealer booths. Located on the historic Last Chance Gulch, the mall offers an extensive selection of furniture, glassware, vintage clothing, jewelry, tools, and eclectic collectibles from traditional to unique styles. Golden Girls Website, Southwest Montana
attractions.address: 505 N Last Chance Gulch, Helena, MT 59601
attractions.hours: Monday - Saturday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Sunday: 12:00 PM - 4:00 PM

attractions.name: Hauser Lake Recreation Area
attractions.description: Hauser Lake Recreation Area is a popular destination on the 3,200-acre Hauser Reservoir, an impoundment of the Missouri River northeast of Helena. The area features several sites including White Sandy, Black Sandy State Park, and Devil's Elbow, offering opportunities for boating, fishing, swimming, and camping against the backdrop of the Big Belt Mountains. It is renowned for its walleye and trout fishing, as well as its scenic views and well-maintained facilities.
attractions.hours: Day-use areas: 7:00 AM - 10:00 PM daily. Campgrounds: Open 24 hours (Check-in 2:00 PM, Check-out 11:00 AM).
attractions.admission: Day-use: $5 per vehicle (BLM areas); Montana Residents: Free (with state park fee paid with vehicle registration); Nonresidents: $8 Day Use Entrance Fee (with vehicle), $4 Walk-in/Bicycle/Bus. Camping: $25 per night per camping unit.
attractions.phone: 406-533-7600 (BLM Butte Field Office), 406-458-3221 (Black Sandy State Park)
highlights: ['Excellent opportunities for boating, water-skiing, and tubing on the 3,200-acre reservoir.', 'Renowned fishing spot for walleye, rainbow trout, and brown trout.', "Includes multiple campgrounds like White Sandy, Black Sandy, and Devil's Elbow with varied amenities.", 'Scenic views of the Big Belt Mountains and Eldorado Bar.', 'Swimming beaches and picnic shelters available for day-use visitors.', 'Access to various hiking and mountain biking trails in the surrounding area.']
attractions.tips: Campsites at popular spots like Black Sandy and White Sandy often require advanced reservations, especially during summer weekends. Be aware of aquatic invasive species regulations and ensure all watercraft are 'Clean, Drain, Dry' before launching. Fire restrictions may be in place during dry summer months, so check current status before visiting. Bureau of Land Management, Montana FWP

attractions.admission: Varies by event; typical ticket prices range from $27.50 to $332+ depending on the performance and seating tier (City of Helena). Public entry to common areas is free during business hours (Tripadvisor).
highlights: 'Unique Moorish Revival architecture featuring a prominent minaret and elaborate tile work ([Wikipedia).', 'Historic 1,925-seat Auditorium Theater used for concerts, ballet, and community theater (City of Helena).', 'Expansive 15,000 sq. ft. Ballroom exhibition hall for banquets and trade shows (City of Helena).', 'Listed on the National Register of Historic Places as the Algeria Shrine Temple (Wikipedia).', "Designated 'Selfie Spot' near the flagpole on the south plaza for iconic photos (Tripadvisor)."]

attractions.name: Original Governor's Mansion
attractions.description: Built in 1888 by Helena entrepreneur William Chessman, this ornate Queen Anne-style residence served as the official home for nine Montana governors and their families from 1913 to 1959. Today, the mansion is preserved as a historic house museum by the Montana Historical Society, offering a glimpse into the state's political and social history through its beautifully restored rooms and period furnishings (Montana Historical Society, Visit Montana).
attractions.hours: Summer (June 1 – Sept 15): Tue-Sat 12:00 PM – 4:00 PM (Tours at 12, 1, 2, and 3 PM), Closed Sun-Mon; Winter (Sept 16 – May 31): Sat 12:00 PM – 4:00 PM (Tours at 12, 1, 2, and 3 PM), Closed Sun-Fri (Montana Historical Society, Southwest Montana)
attractions.days_open: Summer (June 1 – Sept 15): Tuesday–Saturday; Winter (Sept 16 – May 31): Saturdays only (Visit Montana, Montana Historical Society)
attractions.admission: Free for standard public tours (first-come, first-served); $100 for scheduled commercial group tours (Montana Historical Society)
highlights: 'Stunning Queen Anne-style architecture featuring a three-story brick structure and a large wrap-around porch ([Tripadvisor)', "Period-accurate restoration showcasing the living quarters and lifestyles of Montana's early executive families (WhichMuseum)", 'Historic two-story brick carriage house located on the mansion grounds (news.mt.gov)', 'Original interior details including ornate woodwork, stained glass, and authentic late 19th and early 20th-century furnishings (NBC Montana)', 'Informative guided tours led by knowledgeable docents who share personal stories of the governors who lived there (Airial Travel)']
attractions.tips: As of May 2026, the mansion is temporarily closed for porch and balustrade renovations with a planned reopening in July 2026. When open, tours are limited to 20 people on a first-come, first-served basis, so it is recommended to arrive early. Please note the historic building is not ADA compliant and requires navigating multiple flights of stairs (Montana Historical Society, news.mt.gov).

attractions.name: Sleeping Giant Wilderness Study Area
attractions.description: The Sleeping Giant Wilderness Study Area is a 6,666-acre non-motorized recreation area known for its distinctive mountain profile that resembles a reclining human. Managed by the Bureau of Land Management, it features rugged topography, evergreen forests, and 12 miles of shoreline along the Missouri River and Holter Lake. Visitors can enjoy primitive camping, hiking, and wildlife viewing in a landscape that also preserves a portion of the Lewis and Clark National Historic Trail.
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily (Wikipedia)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Montana)
highlights: 'Iconic "Sleeping Giant" rock formation visible from Helena and the Missouri River ([Visit Montana)', 'Over 12 miles of scenic shoreline along Holter Lake (Bureau of Land Management)', 'Seven miles of ridge line hiking routes offering panoramic Rocky Mountain views (Wikipedia)', 'Thriving wildlife populations including mountain goats, bighorn sheep, and bald eagles (Bureau of Land Management)', 'Historical significance as part of the Lewis and Clark National Historic Trail (Visit Montana)', 'Access to approximately 40 primitive boat-in campsites along the lake (Southwest Montana)']
attractions.tips: Trails can be difficult to follow in grassy meadows; carry a map and GPS for navigation. High-clearance vehicles are strongly recommended for the Woodsiding Road access, especially in wet conditions. The BLM advises against taking pets into the area due to rugged terrain and wildlife risks. Boat access from Holter Lake is one of the most popular ways to reach the interior (Bureau of Land Management, Visit Montana).

attractions.name: Boulder Hot Springs Inn, Spa, & Retreat Center
attractions.description: Boulder Hot Springs Inn, Spa, and Retreat Center is a historic landmark nestled in the Elkhorn Mountains of Montana, offering a sanctuary for healing and community. The property is a smoke-free, alcohol-free, and drug-free environment that utilizes geothermal waters to heat its facilities and fill its pools. Guests can enjoy a natural experience with historic themed rooms, healthy cuisine, and access to therapeutic mineral waters. Boulder Hot Springs, Visit Montana
attractions.hours: Daily: 10:00 AM — 8:00 PM (Day Use). Boulder Hot Springs
attractions.admission: Weekday (Mon-Fri): $12 Adults, $10 Seniors (60+), $7 Children (3-12). Weekend & Holiday: $14 Adults, $12 Seniors (60+), $8 Children (3-12). Boulder Hot Springs
highlights: 'Natural geothermal indoor plunges maintained at 103-106°F and an outdoor pool. [Boulder Hot Springs', 'Historic Victorian-era landmark with themed Bed & Breakfast rooms and ghost stories. Visit Montana, Montana Secretary of State', 'Situated on 274 acres bordering the Deer Lodge National Forest with extensive hiking trails. Montana Secretary of State', 'Geothermally heated greenhouse and chicken coop supporting sustainable living. Southwest Montana', 'Alcohol, drug, and smoke-free environment focused on wellness and active living. Boulder Hot Springs']
attractions.tips: Swimsuits are required for the outdoor pool and co-ed indoor plunges. Massage therapy and special event buffets require reservations in advance. Be aware of the strictly enforced alcohol-free and smoke-free policy across the entire property. Boulder Hot Springs, Tripadvisor
attractions.nearby: Deer Lodge National Forest trails, Frederick Franck sculpture (20-minute hike), The Heritage Center, Veterans Park, Elkhorn Ghost Mining Town, Radon Health Mines (Earth Angel, Sunshine), Tizer Botanical Gardens. Boulder Hot Springs, Discover Jefferson County

attractions.name: A Carousel for Missoula
attractions.description: A Carousel for Missoula is a volunteer-built, hand-carved carousel that serves as a community treasure in the heart of downtown. Opened in 1995, it features 38 intricately detailed ponies and two chariots, all housed in a dedicated building. The project was inspired by a local cabinetmaker and brought to life through thousands of hours of volunteer craftsmanship and community donations. (Official Website, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Fall, Winter & Spring (Sept – Mid-June): 11:00 am – 6:00 pm Daily. Summer (Mid-June – Labor Day): 11:00 am – 7:00 pm Daily. (Official Website)
attractions.days_open: Open daily, year-round (except Christmas Day and approximately 12 days in January for maintenance). (Official Website, Yelp)
attractions.admission: Children (11 & under): $1; Riders (12 & older): $2; Riders with disabilities: Always free. Free admission to look at the carousel and play in the adjacent Dragon Hollow park. (Official Website, Atlas Obscura)
highlights: 'Features 38 unique hand-carved horses and two chariots. ([Visit Montana)', 'Home to the largest carousel band organ in continuous use in America. (Wikipedia)', 'The "Brass Ring" game where riders can win a free ride by grabbing the golden ring. (Tripadvisor)', 'Adjacent to Dragon Hollow, a large, magical community-built playground. (Official Website)', 'Enclosed in a climate-controlled building with roll-down doors for year-round enjoyment. (Wikipedia)', 'Available for private party rentals and special events like birthdays and weddings. (Official Website)']
attractions.tips: Riders with disabilities ride for free. Check for seasonal "Out to Lunch" events in the park on Thursdays during the summer for live music and food vendors. Visit the gift shop for unique toys and a DVD history of the carousel. (Official Website, Visit Montana)
attractions.nearby: Caras Park, Clark Fork River, Wilma Theatre, Missoula Art Museum, Dragon Hollow Play Area, Downtown Missoula shopping and dining. (Wikipedia, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Anaconda Smoke Stack State Park (Montana FWP)
attractions.description: Anaconda Smoke Stack State Park preserves one of the tallest free-standing brick structures in the world, a 585-foot chimney completed in 1919 for the Anaconda Copper Company. This massive landmark is listed on the National Register of Historic Places and serves as a monument to Montana's industrial mining heritage. Visitors can view the stack from a designated interpretive site that highlights its historical significance (Montana FWP, Visit Montana).
attractions.hours: Daylight hours (Montana State Parks Foundation)
attractions.admission: Free (Montana FWP)
highlights: "World's tallest surviving masonry structure at 585 feet tall ([Wikipedia)", 'Constructed in just 144 days in 1918 and placed into operation in 1919 (Wikipedia)', 'Internal diameter measures 75 feet at the base and 60 feet at the top (Visit Montana)', 'The stack is 30 feet taller than the Washington Monument, which could fit inside it (Montana State Parks Foundation)', "Designated as a state park in 1986 after local residents organized to 'Save the Stack' (Wikipedia)", "Features a viewing area with interpretive signs detailing the stack's history (Visit Montana)"]
attractions.tips: The stack is located on a Superfund cleanup site and is not accessible for close-up viewing due to soil contamination; use the viewing center at the junction of Park Street and Monroe Street for the best vantage point (Visit Montana). Binoculars are recommended for seeing the details of the brickwork from a distance (Airial Travel). There are no staff or visitor center services on-site (Montana FWP).
attractions.nearby: Old Works Golf Course, Washoe Theater, Copper Village Museum & Art Center, Lost Creek State Park, Georgetown Lake, Smelter City Brewing (Visit Montana, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Berkeley Pit
attractions.description: The Berkeley Pit is a massive former open-pit copper mine in Butte, Montana, that has transformed into a one-mile-long toxic lake since mining ceased in 1982. Visitors can observe the acidic, metal-rich water from a dedicated viewing platform reached via a unique tunnel. The site serves as a stark reminder of the city's mining heritage as the "Richest Hill on Earth" and its ongoing status as a major federal Superfund site. PitWatch, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 350 Shields Ave, Butte, MT 59701 Visit Butte Montana
attractions.region: Silver Bow County, Southwest Montana Southwest Montana Tourism
attractions.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Tuesday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Wednesday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Thursday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Friday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Saturday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Sunday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM PitWatch
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday (Seasonally: mid-May through October) PitWatch
attractions.admission: $3.00 Adults; $2.00 Children/Students (Fees may vary by season or age) Southwest Montana Tourism, Visit The USA
highlights: 'Massive 1,780-foot deep toxic lake with distinctive reddish-gold water due to high mineral content [NASA Earth Observatory', 'Historic viewing tunnel that leads visitors from the gift shop to the main observation deck Roadside America', 'Interpretive pavilion featuring educational displays on mining history, geology, and environmental remediation PitWatch', 'Sophisticated bird-deterrent systems using acoustic sounds to prevent wildlife from landing in the toxic water Wikipedia', 'Panoramic views of the adjacent active Continental Pit mine, which is still in operation today Montana Tech', 'On-site gift shop offering unique local copper-themed souvenirs and handcrafted jewelry Visit Butte Montana']
attractions.tips: Plan your visit between late May and September for the best chance of the viewing stand being open, as it is weather-dependent. The site is a quick but impactful 20-30 minute stop, and the staff at the gift shop are very knowledgeable about Butte's local mining history. Visit Butte Montana, Roadside America
attractions.nearby: World Museum of Mining, Our Lady of the Rockies, Copper King Mansion, Granite Mountain Speculator Mine Memorial Visit Butte Montana

attractions.name: Brennan's Wave
attractions.description: Brennan's Wave is a renowned man-made whitewater feature located on the Clark Fork River in the heart of downtown Missoula. Constructed in 2006 to honor the legacy of world-class kayaker Brennan Guth, it has become a premier destination for river surfing, kayaking, and paddleboarding. The site attracts both dedicated local athletes and spectators who watch the action from the nearby Riverfront Terrace and the Higgins Avenue Bridge. Downtown Missoula Partnership, The Pulp
attractions.region: Downtown Missoula / Caras Park City of Missoula
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily; daylight hours recommended for viewing/activity Airial Travel
attractions.days_open: Daily (365 days a year) Airial Travel
attractions.admission: Free Downtown Missoula Partnership, Airial Travel
highlights: 'Memorial dedicated to Missoula kayaker Brennan Guth [Downtown Missoula Partnership', 'Newly renovated Riverfront Terrace with ADA access and seating Missoula Downtown Partnership', 'World-class river surfing and whitewater kayaking waves Visit Montana', 'Prime viewing area from the Higgins Avenue Bridge Airial Travel', 'Hub for community events like "Downtown ToNight" in adjacent Caras Park Epic7Travel', 'Functional irrigation structure integrated into the recreational feature Paddling Magazine']
attractions.tips: Always check river flow levels (CFS) before surfing as conditions change rapidly with snowmelt; the water is very cold even in summer, so a wetsuit is highly recommended. Follow local surfer etiquette by waiting your turn on the island or bank, and be aware of hidden rocks and currents. For the best photos, head to the Higgins Avenue Bridge overlook. Riverbreak Magazine, Airial Travel
attractions.nearby: Caras Park, A Carousel for Missoula, Dragon Hollow Playasaurus Park, The Wilma Theatre, Missoula Art Museum Downtown Missoula Partnership, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Butte-Anaconda National Historic Landmark District
attractions.description: The Butte-Anaconda National Historic Landmark District is one of the largest and most significant historic districts in the United States, encompassing nearly 6,000 contributing properties that tell the story of "The Richest Hill on Earth." It preserves the industrial heritage of the late 19th and early 20th centuries, where massive mining headframes stand as sentinels over a cityscape of elegant Victorian mansions and bustling commercial blocks. This "Gibraltar of Unionism" was central to the American labor movement and once produced nearly one-third of the world's copper supply National Park Service, Visit Butte.
attractions.hours: Visitor Center (Summer, June-Sept): Mon-Sat 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM, Sun 8:30 AM - 4:00 PM; Visitor Center (Winter, Oct-May): Mon-Fri 8:30 AM - 5:00 PM Butte Convention and Visitor Bureau
attractions.days_open: Visitor Center: Monday-Sunday (Summer), Monday-Friday (Winter); District: Always Open
attractions.admission: General District: Free; Butte Trolley Tour: Adults $25.00, Seniors (65+)/Military $20.00, Students (12-18) $15.00, Children (6-11) $8.00, 5 and under Free Butte Chamber of Commerce; World Museum of Mining: Adults $12.00 World Museum of Mining
highlights: 'Largest National Historic Landmark District in the United States with nearly 6,000 contributing structures [Wikipedia', '14 iconic steel mining headframes (Gallows Frames) that dot the city skyline Visit Butte', 'World Museum of Mining, located on the actual mine yard of the Orphan Girl Mine World Museum of Mining', 'Historic Uptown Butte, featuring spectacular Victorian-era mansions and commercial architecture Mainstreet Butte', "The Berkeley Pit, a massive former open-pit mine that is now one of the world's largest Superfund sites Visit Butte", 'Copper King Mansion, the lavish 34-room former residence of tycoon W.A. Clark Southwest Montana']
attractions.tips: Begin your tour at the Butte Chamber of Commerce Visitor Center for maps and trolley reservations. Wear comfortable walking shoes for exploring the steep streets of Uptown Butte. Be sure to try a traditional Butte pasty, a meat-and-potato pie that was a staple for local miners. Most specialized museums and trolley tours operate primarily from Memorial Day through Labor Day Visit Butte, Southwest Montana.
attractions.nearby: World Museum of Mining, Berkeley Pit, Our Lady of the Rockies, Copper King Mansion, Helena MT (approx. 68 miles north) Visit Butte

attractions.name: C.M. Russell Museum (C.M. Russell Museum Complex)
attractions.description: The C.M. Russell Museum is a premier Western art museum dedicated to the life and legacy of 'cowboy artist' Charles M. Russell. The complex covers an entire city block and houses more than 3,000 pieces of art, including nearly 1,000 creations by Russell himself, alongside his original home and studio. (Official Website, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Summer Hours (May 1 – Sept 30): Monday – Sunday, 10 a.m. – 5 p.m.; Winter Hours (Oct 1 – April 30): Thursday – Monday, 10 a.m. – 5 p.m. (Official Website)
attractions.days_open: Open 7 days a week (Summer); Thursday – Monday (Winter) (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Adults: $20, Seniors (Age 60+): $17, Veterans: $17, Students: $7, Age 5 & Under: Free, Active Military & Families: Free, Scheduled Tour Groups: $17 (book 2 weeks in advance) (Official Website)
highlights: ['Charles M. Russell’s log cabin studio and original family home', 'Extensive collection of Russell’s paintings, sculptures, and illustrated letters', 'Browning Firearms Collection featuring historical weaponry', "'The Bison: American Icon' exhibit with an interactive buffalo diorama", 'Outdoor sculpture garden with works by Robert Scriver', 'Original back bar from the famous Mint Saloon']
attractions.tips: Allow at least 3 hours to explore the full complex, including the house and studio. Photography is generally permitted without flash, except for specific pieces. Visit during 'The Russell' event in March for free admission days. (Tripadvisor, Visit Great Falls Montana)
attractions.nearby: Giant Springs State Park, Lewis and Clark Interpretive Center, Paris Gibson Square Museum of Art, Rivers Edge Trail, First People's Buffalo Jump State Park (Heritage Inn, Great Falls Subaru)

attractions.name: CSKT Bison Range (formerly National Bison Range)
attractions.description: The CSKT Bison Range is an 18,766-acre wildlife conservation area on the Flathead Indian Reservation, home to approximately 350 bison and diverse wildlife including elk, deer, and bears. Restored to the Confederated Salish and Kootenai Tribes in 2022, it offers self-guided scenic driving tours and hiking trails through intermountain bunchgrass prairie and mountain foothills. CSKT Bison Range, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Summer (May-Oct): Gate 7am-8pm, Visitor Center 7am-7pm; Winter (Nov-April): Gate 8am-6pm, Visitor Center 8am-5pm. Last entry for scenic drives occurs 1-2 hours before gate closure. CSKT Bison Range
attractions.days_open: Daily (Monday – Sunday) except for specific holidays CSKT Bison Range
attractions.admission: Day Pass: $20 per vehicle, Day Pass Van (10+ seats): $35 per vehicle, Commercial Day Pass: $60 per vehicle, Annual Pass: $60 per vehicle, CSKT Tribal Members: Free, Bike Pass: $5.00 CSKT Bison Range, Montana Beyond
highlights: ['Self-guided driving tours along Red Sleep Drive and Prairie Drive', 'Viewing of approximately 350 American bison in their natural habitat', 'Wildlife sightings including elk, mule deer, pronghorn, and black bears', 'Scenic overlooks with views of the Mission Mountains and Flathead Valley', 'Educational Visitor Center with museum exhibits and a gift shop', 'Two designated walking trails: High Point Trail and Grassland Trail']
attractions.tips: Call ahead at least one hour before arrival for road and weather updates, as roads may close unexpectedly. Federal park passes (like America the Beautiful) are no longer accepted; visitors must purchase a tribal pass. Arrive early for the best wildlife viewing opportunities, especially for the 2-hour Red Sleep Drive loop. CSKT Bison Range, Montana Beyond
attractions.nearby: Ninepipes Museum of Early Montana, St. Ignatius Mission, Garden of One Thousand Buddhas, Flathead Lake, Ninepipe National Wildlife Refuge Visit Montana, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Dumas Brothel Museum
attractions.description: The Dumas Brothel, built in 1890, stands as the longest-operating house of prostitution in U.S. history, serving the mining community for 92 years until 1982. Now a museum and antique shop, it is the last remaining example of Victorian brothel-style architecture in the country and is listed on the National Register of Historic Places (Tripadvisor, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday: Closed; Wednesday: Closed; Thursday: Closed; Friday: Closed; Saturday: 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM (Every other Saturday); Sunday: Closed (Dumas Brothel, Wikivoyage)
attractions.days_open: Every other Saturday; other days by appointment or for special events (Dumas Brothel)
attractions.admission: $15 - $20 for general tours (prices reported vary due to ongoing restoration efforts); $100 for private paranormal investigations (Yelp, Chilling Paranormal)
highlights: 'Longest-running active brothel in U.S. history (1890–1982) ([Dumas Brothel)', 'Listed on the National Register of Historic Places since 1970 (Wikipedia)', "Features original underground 'cribs' and tunnels once used by miners (Southwest Montana)", 'Last vestige of Victorian Brothel style architecture in the United States (Tripadvisor)', "Renowned paranormal site featured on Travel Channel's 'Ghosts of Devil's Perch' (Dumas Brothel)", "Contains a collection of original artifacts, furniture, and 'madam's quarters' (Dumas Brothel)"]
attractions.tips: Call ahead to confirm the schedule as hours are irregular; the building is under active restoration, so visitors should use caution on stairs and be prepared for a 'work-in-progress' environment (Dumas Brothel, Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: World Museum of Mining, The Mai Wah Museum, Copper King Mansion, Berkeley Pit, Pekin Cafe and Lounge (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Elkhorn Hotsprings Resort
attractions.description: Elkhorn Hotsprings Resort is a rustic, family-owned retreat situated in the scenic Grasshopper Valley of southwest Montana. Since 1918, it has offered guests a primitive getaway featuring mineral-rich geothermal waters, historic lodge accommodations, and homemade country-style dining. (Elkhorn Hotsprings)
attractions.address: 339 Hot Springs Road, Polaris, MT 59746 (Elkhorn Hotsprings)
attractions.region: Grasshopper Valley, Beaverhead-Deerlodge National Forest (Visit Montana)
attractions.hours: Summer: Sun-Thu 8AM-10PM, Fri-Sat 8AM-11PM; Winter: Thu & Sun 8AM-9PM, Fri-Sat 8AM-10PM; limited pool availability on Tue-Wed (Elkhorn Hotsprings)
attractions.days_open: Year-round (daily in summer; Thursday–Sunday in winter with limited pool access on Tuesday/Wednesday) (Elkhorn Hotsprings, Southwest Montana)
attractions.admission: $10 adults, $7 children, $1 towel rental (free for overnight guests); Tuesday Big Pool discount ($5) (Elkhorn Hotsprings)
highlights: 'Two outdoor hot mineral pools naturally heated by geothermal energy ranging from 92-102 degrees ([Elkhorn Hotsprings)', 'Historic 1921 lodge and rustic cabins equipped with wood-burning stoves (Elkhorn Hotsprings)', 'Indoor wet sauna that reaches temperatures of 104-106 degrees (Elkhorn Hotsprings)', 'On-site restaurant and bar serving hearty breakfasts and "homemade country style" meals (Elkhorn Hotsprings)', 'Access to 12 kilometers of groomed cross-country ski trails and 100 miles of snowmobile trails (Visit Montana)', 'Stunning stargazing opportunities in the remote Pioneer Mountains (YouTube)']
attractions.tips: Cell service is nonexistent in the area, though limited wifi is available at the resort. Guests should bring their own towels or rent one for $1; soaking is free for those staying overnight. (Tripadvisor, Elkhorn Hotsprings)
attractions.nearby: Maverick Mountain (skiing), Crystal Park (crystal digging), Big Hole River (fishing), Bannack State Park (ghost town) (Visit Montana, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Flathead Lake State Park
attractions.description: Flathead Lake State Park encompasses the largest natural freshwater lake in the western United States, offering over 200 square miles of pristine water and 185 miles of shoreline. The park consists of six unique units—Big Arm, Finley Point, Wayfarers, West Shore, Wild Horse Island, and Yellow Bay—each providing diverse opportunities for camping, hiking, boating, and wildlife viewing. Visitors can enjoy spectacular vistas of the Swan and Mission Mountains while exploring ecosystems ranging from mature forests to dry prairies. Montana FWP, Visit Montana
attractions.address: 8600 Montana HWY 35 (Wayfarers Unit) or 490 North Meridian Road (Central Office)
attractions.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 7:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 7:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Thursday: 7:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Friday: 7:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Saturday: 7:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Sunday: 7:00 AM - 10:00 PM (Note: Camping is 24/7; day-use hours may vary by unit). Tripadvisor
attractions.admission: Montana Residents: Free (with $9 annual vehicle registration fee); Non-residents: $8.00 per vehicle, $4.00 for walk-ins/cyclists; Non-resident 7-Day Pass: $35.00; Non-resident Annual Pass: $50.00. Montana FWP
highlights: ['Boating and sailing on the expansive waters of the largest freshwater lake west of the Mississippi River.', 'Exploring Wild Horse Island, a primitive day-use park home to bighorn sheep, mule deer, and wild horses.', 'World-class fishing for trophy-sized lake trout, whitefish, and yellow perch.', 'Hiking the rocky outcrops and scenic trails of the West Shore unit for panoramic mountain views.', 'Sampling fresh local cherries from roadside orchards along the east shore during the summer months.', "Camping at one of the 140 available sites across five of the park's lakefront units."]
attractions.tips: The lake water remains quite cold year-round, so be prepared for chilly temperatures when swimming. If you plan to fish or recreate on the southern half of the lake, you must purchase a Tribal Recreation Permit as it is part of the Flathead Indian Reservation. Campsites are highly popular and should be reserved well in advance through the official state parks website. Montana FWP, Hike Bike Travel
attractions.nearby: Glacier National Park, National Bison Range, Bigfork Summer Playhouse, Jewel Basin Hiking Area, Lone Pine State Park. Visit Montana, Discover Kalispell

attractions.name: Giant Springs State Park
attractions.description: Giant Springs State Park features one of the largest freshwater springs in the country, producing over 156 million gallons of water daily at a constant 54 degrees. Discovered by the Lewis and Clark Expedition in 1805, the park offers over 30 miles of trails, a fish hatchery, and access to the world's shortest river, the Roe River. Montana FWP
attractions.hours: Sunrise - Sunset Montana FWP
attractions.admission: Montana Residents with $9 state parks fee on vehicle registration: Free; Nonresidents with vehicle: $8; Nonresidents walk-in/bicycle/bus passenger: $4; Nonresidents with Entrance Pass: Free. Montana FWP
highlights: ['Giant Springs (156 million gallons daily)', "Roe River (once world's shortest river)", 'Giant Springs State Fish Hatchery', "River's Edge Trail System (30+ miles of park trails)", 'Four Missouri River waterfalls within the park', 'Lewis and Clark Interpretive Center (neighboring)']
attractions.tips: The park is day-use only (no camping). In winter, steam rises off the unfrozen water, while in summer, the area is notably cooler than the city. Visit the show pond at the fish hatchery to feed the fish. Montana FWP
attractions.nearby: Lewis and Clark Interpretive Center, Rainbow Falls Overlook, Missouri River, Great Falls of the Missouri. Montana FWP

attractions.name: Glacier National Park NPS
attractions.description: Glacier National Park preserves over 1 million acres of glacier-carved peaks and valleys in Montana's Rocky Mountains, extending to the Canadian border. It is famously home to the 50-mile Going-to-the-Sun Road, which provides access to spectacular alpine scenery, diverse wildlife, and over 700 miles of hiking trails. NPS
attractions.hours: Park: Open 24 hours daily. Visitor Centers: Generally 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM daily (seasonal, mid-May through early October). AllTrips, NPS Visitor Centers
attractions.admission: Private Vehicle: $35.00, Motorcycle: $30.00, Individual (on foot/bicycle): $20.00. Non-US residents (16+) must pay an additional $100 per person fee. NPS Fees
highlights: 'Going-to-the-Sun Road [Visit Montana', 'Logan Pass Visitor Center NPS', 'Lake McDonald Visit Montana', 'Many Glacier Valley Reddit', 'Hidden Lake Overlook Wikipedia)', 'Avalanche Lake Trail Gather and Go']
attractions.tips: Arrive early (before 8 AM) to secure parking at popular spots like Logan Pass. No vehicle reservations are required for the 2026 season. Always carry bear spray and check current weather/road conditions before traveling. NPS Alerts, Reddit
attractions.nearby: Flathead National Forest, Whitefish Mountain Resort, Flathead Lake, Waterton Lakes National Park (Canada), Polebridge Mercantile Visit Montana, Under Canvas

attractions.name: Grant-Kohrs Ranch National Historic Site
attractions.description: Grant-Kohrs Ranch National Historic Site commemorates the Western cattle industry from its inception in the 1850s through recent times. Originally established by Canadian fur trader Johnny Grant and later expanded by cattle baron Conrad Kohrs, the 1,618-acre site was once the headquarters of a 10 million acre cattle empire. Today, it remains a working ranch maintained by the National Park Service, offering visitors a glimpse into the life of the American cowboy and the development of the open-range cattle era. (National Park Service, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Summer (May 25–September 7): Mon-Sun 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; Off-season (September–May): Mon-Sun 9:00 AM–4:30 PM (National Park Service)
attractions.days_open: Open daily year-round, except for Thanksgiving, Christmas Day, and New Year's Day (National Park Service)
attractions.admission: Free for all visitors (National Park Service)
highlights: ['Guided tours of the historic 1862-1890 ranch house with original furnishings', 'Living history demonstrations including blacksmithing and seasonal ranch chores', 'Chuckwagon talks centered around open-range life with cowboy coffee provided', 'Preservation of 88 historic buildings including barns, bunkhouses, and sheds', 'Nearly ten miles of ranch roads and trails, such as the Nature Trail and Old Milwaukee Trail', 'A working cattle ranch experience with active grazing and haying operations']
attractions.tips: Stop at the visitor center immediately upon arrival to sign up for house tours, as they are limited-capacity and the only way to view the interior. Wear comfortable walking shoes to explore the extensive grounds and trails. Please respect regulations by keeping pets and smoking restricted to the parking lot area. (National Park Service)
attractions.nearby: Old Prison Museum, Hillcrest Cemetery, William K. Kohrs Memorial Library, Big Hole National Battlefield, Montana Historical Society Museum (Helena) (National Park Service)

attractions.name: Great Falls Portage National Historic Landmark
attractions.description: The Great Falls Portage is a National Historic Landmark commemorating the 18-mile route taken by the Lewis and Clark Expedition in 1805 to bypass five massive waterfalls on the Missouri River. This arduous 31-day journey involved transporting heavy equipment overland on crude wagons and is interpreted today through exhibits and trails at the Lewis and Clark National Historic Trail Interpretive Center. National Park Service, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 4201 Giant Springs Road (Lewis and Clark Interpretive Center)
attractions.hours: Summer: Daily 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Winter: Wednesday–Sunday 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM Central Montana Tourism
attractions.admission: Adults (16+): $8.00; Children (15 & younger): Free; Federal Interagency Passes accepted. Lewis & Clark Foundation
highlights: 'Interactive exhibits at the Lewis and Clark National Historic Trail Interpretive Center [Lewis & Clark Interpretive Center', 'Views of the five historic waterfalls (Great Falls, Crooked, Rainbow, Colter, and Black Eagle) Epic7Travel', 'Giant Springs, one of the largest freshwater springs in the world, located adjacent to the interpretive center NPS', 'The Lower Portage Staging Area and Upper Portage Camp at White Bear Islands Wikipedia', 'Interpretive walking trails following segments of the original 18-mile portage route Lewis and Clark Trail Experience', 'The two-story diorama depicting the struggle of the portage at the visitor center Leisure Group Travel']
attractions.tips: Visit the Ryan Dam overlook to see the actual 'Great Falls' of the Missouri River. Most of the original portage route is on private land, so the best way to experience the history is via the Interpretive Center and public overlooks. Allow at least 2-3 hours for the museum exhibits and nearby Giant Springs. Leisure Group Travel, NPS
attractions.nearby: Giant Springs State Park, C.M. Russell Museum, Roe River (world's shortest river), First Peoples Buffalo Jump State Park, and Ryan Dam (The Great Falls). Epic7Travel

attractions.name: Historical Museum at Fort Missoula
attractions.description: The Historical Museum at Fort Missoula is a 32-acre historic site dedicated to preserving and interpreting the history of Missoula County and western Montana. Established in 1975, it features over 20 preserved historic buildings, including an 1877 military post, a WWII internment camp for Italian and Japanese nationals, and exhibits on the local forest products industry (Official Website).
attractions.hours: Labor Day to Memorial Day: Tuesday–Sunday 12:00 PM – 5:00 PM; Memorial Day to Labor Day: Monday–Saturday 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Sunday 12:00 PM – 5:00 PM (Official Website).
attractions.days_open: Tuesday–Sunday (Closed Monday) year-round; Open Mondays Memorial Day through Labor Day (Official Website).
attractions.admission: Adults: $5.00, Seniors: $4.00, Students: $3.00, Max Per Family: $15.00, Free for Missoula County Residents, Members, and children under 6. Grounds are always open and free (Official Website).
highlights: 'Over 20 preserved historic buildings including an old schoolhouse, depot, and church ([AllMissoula).', "Exhibits on the 25th Infantry Bicycle Corps, an all-African American 'Buffalo Soldier' regiment (Official Website).", 'The WWII Alien Detention Center exhibit detailing the internment of over 2,200 Italian and Japanese nationals (Official Website).', 'A 32-acre park featuring walking trails, a steam locomotive, and a fire observation lookout (Official Website).', 'Extensive collections on the timber and forest products industry of Western Montana (Official Website).', 'Annual events like the Old-Fashioned 4th of July Celebration and Forestry Day (Official Website).']
attractions.tips: Visitors should wear comfortable walking shoes as the museum spans a large 32-acre campus with outdoor historical structures. Picking up a map and watching the orientation video at the museum entrance is recommended to plan your route around the grounds (Yelp). Admission is free for all Missoula County residents (Official Website).

attractions.name: James R. Wilkins, Jr. Conservation Education Center (Rocky Mountain Elk Foundation)

Today, it anchors a diverse museum complex where visitors can explore the original cell blocks along with collections featuring over 200 automobiles, Western history artifacts, and antique toys (Old Prison Museum).

attractions.name: Our Lady of the Rockies
attractions.description: Our Lady of the Rockies is a 90-foot tall statue of Mary, the mother of Jesus, standing atop the Continental Divide overlooking Butte, Montana. It was built as a tribute to motherhood and a promise kept by Bob O'Bill following his wife's recovery from cancer. Completed in 1985, the statue is the fourth-tallest in the United States and serves as a non-denominational memorial to all mothers. Our Lady of the Rockies, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Scapegoat Wilderness (part of the Bob Marshall Wilderness Complex)
attractions.description: The Scapegoat Wilderness is a 239,936-acre protected area in the Helena-Lewis and Clark and Lolo National Forests of Montana. Established in 1972, it was the first citizen-led wilderness initiative in the country and is a key component of the 1.5-million-acre Bob Marshall Wilderness Complex. The area is characterized by rugged limestone cliffs, high alpine meadows, and serves as a vital habitat for grizzly bears, mountain goats, and elk. Wikipedia, Visit Montana

Wikipedia', 'Heart Lake: A popular destination for day hikers and backpackers near Indian Meadows. Wild Montana', 'Rare Wildlife: Home to grizzly bears, wolverines, gray wolves, and mountain lions. Visit Montana']
attractions.tips: Visit in July or August for the best trail conditions and to ensure seasonal gates like Copper Creek are open. attractions.name: Washoe Theatre
attractions.description: The Washoe Theatre is a stunning Art Deco (Nuevo Deco) masterpiece opened in 1936 and designed by renowned architect B. Marcus Priteca. Rated as a national treasure by the Smithsonian, the theater is listed on the National Register of Historic Places and continues to provide a classic cinematic experience with modern digital sound and display. Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Monday: 7:00 PM; Tuesday: 7:00 PM; Wednesday: 7:00 PM; Thursday: 7:00 PM; Friday: 7:00 PM; Saturday: 5:00 PM; Sunday: 5:00 PM Official Website
attractions.admission: Regular: Adults $7.00, Children (11 & Under) $6.00, SEXTET Night $6.00; 3-D: Adults $9.00, Children (11 & Under) $8.00, 3-D OCTET Night $8.00; Free Saturday Matinees (periodically) Official Website
highlights: ["Listed on the National Register of Historic Places for its unique 'Nuevo Deco' architectural style.", 'Features a lavish interior with murals by Colville Smythe, silver and gold leaf accents, and ornamental copper ironwork.', "Renowned for near-perfect acoustics and was a showcase for Western Electric's 'Mirrophonic Sound' innovation.", 'Includes a historic silk curtain painted with deer stags, considered a significant piece of art.', 'Maintains original seating and a classic balcony for a nostalgic movie-going atmosphere.', 'Designated as a national treasure by the Smithsonian Institution.']
attractions.tips: Arrive early to appreciate the intricate lobby murals and ornate Art Deco details. Please note that while the main floor is accessible, the balcony requires climbing stairs. Concessions are reasonably priced, offering classic theater snacks like popcorn and candy. Airial Travel
attractions.nearby: Kennedy Commons (Ice Skating Rink), Hearst Free Library, Copper Village Museum and Art Center, Anaconda Smelter Stack State Park, Old Works Golf Course, Washoe Park Discover Anaconda

attractions.name: Whitefish Mountain Resort
attractions.description: Whitefish Mountain Resort is a premier year-round destination in northwest Montana, renowned for its laid-back atmosphere and stunning views of Glacier National Park. The resort features over 3,000 acres of skiable terrain in the winter and transforms into an adventure hub in the summer with zip lines, an alpine slide, and world-class mountain biking. (Whitefish Mountain Resort, Tripadvisor)
attractions.hours: Summer: 10 a.m. – 5:30 p.m. (daily operations mid-June to early Sept); Winter: 9 a.m. – 4 p.m. (lifts typically open 9:30 a.m. – 4 p.m.). Base Lodge: 9 a.m. – 5 p.m. Mon-Fri. (Whitefish Mountain Resort)
attractions.days_open: Daily during peak winter (early Dec - mid April) and peak summer (mid June - early Sept) seasons; weekends during early/late summer shoulder seasons (late May - early June, mid Sept). (Whitefish Mountain Resort)
attractions.admission: Winter Lift Tickets: Adults (19-64) $115, Seniors (65-69) $99, Super Seniors (70+) $45, Teens (13-18) $99, Juniors (7-12) $58, children (6 & younger) FREE. Summer Activities: Scenic Lift Ride $34 (Adult), Alpine Slide $14 (1 ride), Zip Line Tour $92, Aerial Adventure Park $62 (Adult), Spider Monkey Mountain $12. Summer Activity Packages available from $95-$123. (Whitefish Mountain Resort, Summer Activities)
highlights: ['Over 3,000 acres of terrain with 111 named trails and 11 chairlifts for all skill levels.', 'Panoramic summit views overlooking Glacier National Park and the Flathead Valley.', "Montana's longest lift-served Zip Line Tour featuring spans up to 1,900 feet.", 'Extensive Whitefish Bike Park with over 20 miles of lift-served downhill trails.', 'Unique summer attractions including the Alpine Slide and the Aerial Adventure Park with 60+ obstacles.', "The Summit House, Montana's only mountaintop restaurant, accessible via scenic lift rides."]
attractions.tips: Book lift tickets and activities online at least 48 hours in advance to save up to 10-33%. The free S.N.O.W. Bus provides easy transportation between downtown Whitefish and the mountain during winter. Visit the Summit House for the best photographic views of the region. (Whitefish Mountain Resort)
attractions.nearby: Glacier National Park (West Entrance), Downtown Whitefish, Whitefish Lake, Whitefish Lake Golf Club, Flathead National Forest. (Whitefish Mountain Resort, Visit Montana)

attractions.name: World Museum of Mining
attractions.description: The World Museum of Mining is a 22-acre outdoor museum and memorial built atop the inactive Orphan Girl Mine. It features over 50 historic structures, including a replica 1890s mining town named Hell Roarin’ Gulch, and offers guided underground tours that descend 100 feet into the mine. World Museum of Mining, Wikipedia
attractions.region: Uptown Butte, near Montana Tech campus. Wikipedia, World Museum of Mining
attractions.hours: Peak Season (April 1 - October 30): Daily 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Off-Season (January 15 - March 31): Wednesday - Saturday 11:00 AM - 3:30 PM (Sunday 10:30 AM - 4:00 PM in April). World Museum of Mining
attractions.days_open: Seven days a week (April 1 – October 30); Wednesday – Saturday (January 15 – March 31); Closed January 1 – 14. World Museum of Mining
attractions.admission: General Admission: Adults $12.00, Seniors (65+) $10.00, Youth (5-12) $10.00, Children (0-4) Free; Underground Mine Tours: $30.00 (Tuesdays $25.00), includes General Admission. World Museum of Mining, WhichMuseum
highlights: 'Orphan Girl Mine Yard with its original 100-foot steel headframe and hoist house. [World Museum of Mining', 'Hell Roarin’ Gulch, a meticulously re-created 1890s mining town with over 50 buildings and authentic artifacts. Visit Butte Montana', 'Guided Underground Mine Tour descending 100 feet into the Orphan Girl Mine to see an exposed vein. Southwest Montana', 'Extensive collection of mining machinery and equipment used from the 1860s to the 1970s. World Museum of Mining', 'Photo archive containing over 22,000 historical images depicting Butte’s mining legacy. WeddingWire']
attractions.tips: Reservations for underground tours are strongly recommended as space is limited and they often sell out. Closed-toed shoes are mandatory for the underground tour; Crocs and sandals are not permitted. The museum is primarily outdoors, so dress for the weather. World Museum of Mining, Tripadvisor
